title: agrostis borealis var. recta
intro: Agrostis borealis var. recta, also known as Northern bentgrass, is a perennial grass native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Eurasia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Northern bentgrass include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Northern bentgrass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and roots can be used in traditional folk remedies and modern herbal medicine.
